channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600, leaves others buried
item.description: The 7.7 magnitude quake hit midday Friday with an epicenter near Mandalay, bringing down scores of buildings and damaging other infrastructure like the city’s airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: See a missing police officer post? It's likely part of a nationwide scam, authorities say
item.description: Have you seen a post on Facebook recently, asking you to be on the lookout for a missing police officer? Chances are, you're actually seeing a scam post, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Calalang, KRON4

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Thousands of people have no power in Wisconsin and northern Michigan after freezing rain brought down trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Roki Sasaki pulled from Dodger Stadium debut in 2nd inning after more struggles with his control
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Roki Sasaki's career with the Los Angeles Dodgers is not off to a sensational start. For a young pitcher who's been a dominant force at every other step in his meteoric baseball career, that's both a surprise and a challenge.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender people are about 1% of the US population. Yet they're a political lightning rod
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their fights often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump-affirmed Secret Service agent 'DJ' Daniel continues record push to be sworn in by 1,000 law agencies
item.description: Thirteen-year-old DJ Daniel, the boy with brain cancer sworn in by President Donald Trump earlier this year as an honorary Secret Service agent, is on his way to setting a world record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Death of 90s sitcom actress being investigated by homicide detectives: report
item.description: Per TMZ, the woman has been identified as 58-year-old actress Cindyana Santangelo, best known for a role in "Married With Children.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds have protested billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 current, former Texas deputies from same sheriff's office die by suicide just weeks apart: officials
item.description: Four current and former deputies from the same sheriff's office in Texas have died by apparent suicide over a six-week period, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender teens fight back against Trump's ban on participation in girls' sports
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Parker Tirrell, 16, enjoys her art classes, scrolling on TikTok and working at her new job at a pet store. But most of all, the transgender teen loves playing soccer. Until last year, that wasn’t a problem.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Satanic Grotto leader taken away in handcuffs at the Kansas Statehouse
item.description: TOPEKA (KSNT) - Law enforcement officers arrested the leader of a satanic organization Friday morning along with a handful of others at the Kansas Statehouse who were present to participate in or protest against a "Black Mass" ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about a potentially toxic mix of worsening inflation and a U.S. economy slowing because of households afraid to spend due to the global trade war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Social media influencer promotes clean eating amid federal push to ban food dyes
item.description: As efforts to ban the use of synthetic dyes in food gain ground in several states across the United States, a social media influencer is using her platform to help people become more aware of the harmful ingredients in their food.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawyer for Michigan couple held in Mexican prison says resort company is at fault
item.description: The lawyer for a Michigan couple in a Mexican prison accused of breaching a timeshare contract said the resort that filed the criminal complaint breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4
